content.text: Artifact promotion is the practice of taking a single built artifact — a Docker image identified by its immutable SHA256 digest — and advancing it through a sequence of environments (dev → staging → pre-production → production), where each environment transition requires passing a gate. Gates prevent artifacts that pass a minimal check in dev from reaching production without surviving increasingly rigorous validation.

For GenAI applications, promotion gates are more complex than for conventional applications because eval suites are expensive, non-deterministic, and must be scoped differently at each stage to balance speed against thoroughness.

attrs.alt: Artifact promotion gates across dev, staging, pre-prod, and production

content.text: The artifact built in CI — the Docker image — must not be rebuilt at any later stage. Rebuilding reintroduces non-determinism: dependency resolution may pick different package versions, the base image may have been updated, or environment variables may differ subtly. Instead, the same image digest is

# Build once, record the digest
docker build -t myregistry/myapp:1.3.2 .
DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' myregistry/myapp:1.3.2 | cut -d@ -f2)
echo "ARTIFACT_DIGEST=$DIGEST" >> $GITHUB_ENV
docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2

# At each promotion stage, re-tag using the digest — never rebuild
# Dev -> Staging:
docker pull myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$ARTIFACT_DIGEST
docker tag  myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$ARTIFACT_DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-staging
docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-staging

# Staging -> Pre-prod:
docker tag  myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$ARTIFACT_DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-preprod
docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-preprod

# Pre-prod -> Production:
docker tag  myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$ARTIFACT_DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-prod
docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-prod
# Production Kubernetes manifest pins by digest, not tag:
# image: myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@sha256:abc123...
in Kubernetes manifests. Deploying by tag alone allows the registry to swap the underlying image without your knowledge.
The build-to-dev gate is the cheapest gate. It validates that the artifact is structurally sound and not carrying any obvious security risks before it touches any running environment:

gate-1-build-to-dev:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Verify image digest is recorded
        run: |
          [ -n "${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}" ] ||             (echo "No digest — build did not produce an artifact" && exit 1)

      - name: SBOM present
        run: |
          aws s3 ls s3://my-sboms/${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}/sbom.json ||             (echo "SBOM missing for this digest" && exit 1)

      - name: Secrets scan (no findings allowed)
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with: { path: ./, base: ${{ github.event.before }} }

      - name: Version envelope complete
        run: |
          python -c "
import json
e = json.load(open('version_envelope.json'))
required = ['app_version', 'prompt_version', 'model', 'git_sha']
missing = [k for k in required if not e.get(k)]
if missing:
    raise SystemExit(f'Version envelope missing: {missing}')
print('Version envelope OK:', e)
"
      - name: Deploy to dev
        run: kubectl set image deployment/myapp app="myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-dev" -n dev

The dev-to-staging gate runs a sampled eval suite (30% of the golden dataset) to catch major regressions quickly without the full API cost. This gate is still automated — it does not require human approval.

gate-2-dev-to-staging:
    needs: gate-1-build-to-dev
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install openai pytest

      - name: Smoke tests against dev
        run: |
          pytest tests/smoke/ -q             --base-url=https://dev.myapp.example.com

      - name: API schema unchanged
        run: |
          python scripts/check_schema.py             --baseline api/schema_baseline.json             --current https://dev.myapp.example.com/openapi.json

      - name: Sampled eval (30%)
        env: { OPENAI_API_KEY: '${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}' }
        run: |
          python eval/accuracy.py             --dataset eval/datasets/golden_v3.jsonl             --sample-rate 0.3             --judge-model gpt-4o-mini             --gate 0.88             --output gate2_accuracy.json

          python eval/safety.py             --dataset eval/datasets/safety_v2.jsonl             --sample-rate 0.3             --gate 0.99             --output gate2_safety.json

      - name: Promote artifact to staging
        if: success()
        run: |
          DIGEST="${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}"
          docker pull myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$DIGEST
          docker tag  myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-staging
          docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-staging
          kubectl set image deployment/myapp app="myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-staging" -n staging
The staging-to-pre-prod gate runs the full eval suite (100% of the golden dataset, full-size judge model) and includes a latency regression check and a manual review of any open P0 defects:

gate-3-staging-to-preprod:
    needs: gate-2-dev-to-staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install openai pytest locust

      - name: Full eval suite (100%)
        env: { OPENAI_API_KEY: '${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}' }
        run: |
          python eval/accuracy.py             --dataset eval/datasets/golden_v3.jsonl             --sample-rate 1.0             --judge-model gpt-4o-2024-11-20             --gate 0.88             --output gate3_accuracy.json

          python eval/safety.py             --dataset eval/datasets/safety_v2.jsonl             --sample-rate 1.0             --gate 0.99             --output gate3_safety.json

      - name: Latency regression check
        run: |
          python scripts/perf_baseline.py             --endpoint https://staging.myapp.example.com/chat             --requests 200             --gate-pct 1.20 \   # must not exceed 120% of previous p95
            --baseline perf_baselines/last_staging.json             --output gate3_perf.json

      - name: Check for open P0 defects
        run: |
          python scripts/check_open_defects.py             --severity P0             --service myapp             --fail-if-any

      - name: Promote artifact to pre-prod
        if: success()
        run: |
          DIGEST="${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}"
          docker tag myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-preprod
          docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-preprod
          kubectl set image deployment/myapp app="myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-preprod" -n preprod

Gate 4 is the only gate that requires human approval. It ensures a person reviews the combined evidence from all prior gates before live traffic is exposed to the new model behaviour. Use GitHub Environments with a required reviewer list:

gate-4-preprod-to-prod:
    needs: gate-3-staging-to-preprod
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: production            # requires manual approval in GitHub UI
      url: https://myapp.example.com
    steps:
      - name: Verify pre-prod eval results
        run: |
          python scripts/verify_gate_evidence.py             --accuracy gate3_accuracy.json             --safety   gate3_safety.json             --perf     gate3_perf.json             --output   gate4_checklist.md

      - name: Notify safety team
        run: |
          python scripts/notify_safety_team.py             --version "${{ env.APP_VERSION }}"             --prompt-version "${{ env.PROMPT_VERSION }}"             --eval-run-id "${{ env.EVAL_RUN_ID }}"

      - name: Promote to production (blue/green)
        run: |
          DIGEST="${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}"
          docker tag myregistry/myapp:1.3.2@$DIGEST myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-prod
          docker push myregistry/myapp:1.3.2-prod
          bash scripts/blue_green_deploy.sh 1.3.2

      - name: Record promotion in audit log
        run: |
          python scripts/audit_log.py             --event "artifact_promoted_to_production"             --digest "${{ needs.build.outputs.image_digest }}"             --approver "${{ github.actor }}"             --version-envelope version_envelope.json

Every promotion event — including which human approved gate 4, which eval run validated it, and what the image digest was — must be recorded in an append-only audit log for compliance and incident investigation:

# scripts/audit_log.py
"""Append-only promotion audit log."""
import json, pathlib, datetime, argparse, subprocess

AUDIT_LOG = pathlib.Path("audit/promotion_log.jsonl")
AUDIT_LOG.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

def get_git_sha() -> str:
    return subprocess.check_output(["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"], text=True).strip()[:12]

def append(event: str, digest: str, approver: str, envelope: dict):
    entry = {
        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
        "event": event,
        "image_digest": digest,
        "approver": approver,
        "app_version": envelope.get("app_version"),
        "prompt_version": envelope.get("prompt_version"),
        "model": envelope.get("model"),
        "eval_run_id": envelope.get("eval_run_id"),
        "git_sha": envelope.get("git_sha", get_git_sha()),
    }
    with AUDIT_LOG.open("a") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
    print(f"Audit log: {event} by {approver} for {digest[:16]}...")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--event")
    p.add_argument("--digest")
    p.add_argument("--approver")
    p.add_argument("--version-envelope")
    args = p.parse_args()
    envelope = json.load(open(args.version_envelope))
    append(args.event, args.digest, args.approver, envelope)
Occasionally a critical security patch must bypass the full promotion sequence. Define the bypass policy explicitly so it is not abused:
Gate 3 latency check, Gate 3 full eval
Gate 4 manual approval can never be bypassed for any reason. An automated shortcut to production without human sign-off defeats the entire purpose of the promotion pipeline.

Before gate 4 is approved, a verification script consolidates evidence from all prior gates into a human-readable checklist. This gives the approver a single document rather than requiring them to hunt through multiple CI artifacts:

# scripts/verify_gate_evidence.py
"""Consolidate gate evidence into a promotion checklist."""
import json, pathlib, sys, argparse

def check(label: str, condition: bool, detail: str = "") -> dict:
    status = "PASS" if condition else "FAIL"
    print(f"  [{status}] {label}" + (f": {detail}" if detail else ""))
    return {"label": label, "passed": condition, "detail": detail}

def main(accuracy_file, safety_file, perf_file, output):
    accuracy = json.load(open(accuracy_file))
    safety   = json.load(open(safety_file))
    perf     = json.load(open(perf_file))

    checks = [
        check("Accuracy eval >= 88%", accuracy["pass_rate"] >= 0.88,
              f"{accuracy['pass_rate']:.1%}"),
        check("Safety eval >= 99%",   safety["refusal_accuracy"] >= 0.99,
              f"{safety['refusal_accuracy']:.1%}"),
        check("Latency p95 within 20% of baseline",
              perf["p95_ms"] <= perf["prev_p95_ms"] * 1.20,
              f"{perf['p95_ms']}ms vs baseline {perf['prev_p95_ms']}ms"),
        check("No P0 defects open", accuracy.get("open_p0_defects", 0) == 0,
              str(accuracy.get("open_p0_defects", 0))),
        check("Mutation score on record", pathlib.Path("eval/mutation_scores.jsonl").exists()),
    ]

    all_passed = all(c["passed"] for c in checks)
    result = {"all_passed": all_passed, "checks": checks}
    pathlib.Path(output).write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2))
    print(f"\nPromotion gate evidence: {'APPROVED' if all_passed else 'BLOCKED'}")
    if not all_passed:
        sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--accuracy"); p.add_argument("--safety")
    p.add_argument("--perf"); p.add_argument("--output", default="gate4_checklist.json")
    args = p.parse_args()
    main(args.accuracy, args.safety, args.perf, args.output)
Each environment should configure the application identically, with only the scale and external endpoint differing. GenAI-specific configuration to vary per environment:

# config/environments/dev.yaml
llm_model: gpt-4o-mini          # cheaper model for dev experiments
rate_limit_rpm: 10              # conservative to avoid dev budget overrun
eval_sample_rate: 0.1           # 10% of golden dataset in dev smoke tests
shadow_mode: false              # responses served directly (no shadow logging)
log_level: debug
pii_redaction: true

# config/environments/staging.yaml
llm_model: gpt-4o-2024-11-20   # same model as production
rate_limit_rpm: 60
eval_sample_rate: 0.3
shadow_mode: true               # log responses but serve them
log_level: info
pii_redaction: true

# config/environments/production.yaml
llm_model: gpt-4o-2024-11-20
rate_limit_rpm: 500
eval_sample_rate: 1.0           # nightly full eval in prod
shadow_mode: false
log_level: warn
pii_redaction: true
canary_weight: 5                # start at 5% traffic on new deploys
pin must be identical between staging and production — testing against
to production invalidates the staging eval results.

Artifact promotion gates enforce a single, immutable artifact through progressively rigorous validation environments, preventing code that passed a minimal dev check from reaching production without surviving accuracy, safety, performance, and manual review gates.
Build the Docker image once; record the SHA256 digest; re-tag it at each environment transition without rebuilding.
Gate 1 (build to dev): secrets scan, SBOM, version envelope — automated.
Gate 2 (dev to staging): sampled eval (30%), smoke tests, schema check — automated.
Gate 3 (staging to pre-prod): full eval (100%), latency regression, zero open P0 defects — automated.
Gate 4 (pre-prod to prod): manual approval required, safety team notification, audit log entry.
Log every promotion event in an append-only audit trail with digest, approver, and version envelope.
Define explicit bypass policies for emergency scenarios; gate 4 manual approval can never be bypassed.
